description: Allowlist for registry kernels that are intentionally retained even when they are not live in supported text manifests or capability fallback graphs. The supported-manifest report uses this file to separate protected non-text, utility, branch-local, legacy, or experimental lanes from delete candidates.

fields.id: Stable identifier for this allowlist entry.
fields.category: High-level protected lane, such as runtime-utility, js-dispatched, superseded-legacy, dormant-perf, branch-local, training, diffusion, moe, generation, format, or kv-cache.
fields.reason: Why matched variants should not be treated as delete candidates.
fields.owner: Team or subsystem responsible for the retained lane.
fields.created: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) this entry was added.
fields.match: Matcher over registry entries. Supported fields: keys, operations, operationPrefixes, operationSuffixes, variants, variantPrefixes, variantSuffixes, wgsl, wgslPrefixes, wgslSuffixes, reachabilityStatuses.

entries.reason: Generic runtime utility kernels are retained for wrapper-dispatched operations and tests even when no selected text manifest pins them.
Flash prefill split/reduce kernels are dispatched by the attention wrapper through variant flow and are known false positives for selected-manifest-only scans.
Original generic f16/f16-KV attention variants are retained until their replacement coverage and fallback absence are audited explicitly.
Original row-split SwiGLU kernels are retained while fused FFN replacement coverage remains under audit.
Q4 prefill fusion and WideTile experiments are retained as explicit performance work, not selected-release cleanup candidates.
Branch-local W4A16 kernels are retained for the Gemma 4 31B f16 activation investigation lane until the branch is promoted or abandoned.
Backward, loss, optimizer, and gradient helper kernels are retained for experimental training flows, not supported inference manifests.
Conv2D backward input/weight kernels are retained for training and diffusion-gradient flows.
Loss and gradient helper kernels are training-path assets even when no supported manifest pins them.
Image and diffusion pipeline kernels are retained for diffusion subsystem work, not text-model execution graphs.
Diffusion linear-attention kernels are not expected to appear in supported text manifests.
Energy subsystem kernels are intentionally quarantined experimental assets.
MoE dispatch helper kernels are retained for mixture-of-experts model support even without a verified MoE manifest.
Quantized and alternate KV-cache attention kernels are retained for cache layout experiments and capability work.
Sampling option kernels are runtime generation features and may not be pinned by deterministic verification manifests.
Format conversion and dequantization utility kernels are retained for loader, conversion, and fallback paths outside the supported-manifest set.
